author: Abu Naim Muhammad Abdul Azim, Maulvi
title: Aziz-ul-Khataba
gloss: A biographical book giving a brief account of the more famous companions of Muhammad.
copyright: M. Abu Naim Muhammad Abdul Azim, Hyderabad Deccan; No. (?), dated the (?) July 1910.
notes: Author in Hyderabad Deccan, printed at Amritsar — long-distance print network.

flags.issue: register number and day partially legible in copyright column

title: Madam Blavatsky Sahiba ki Swanih-i-Umri
gloss: A lecture delivered at Delhi in May 1910 by Rai Bishambhar Nath, Executive Engineer and Member of Theosophical Society, on the life of Madam Blavatsky, the world-famed Russian votary of Theosophy.
copyright: Rai Bishambhar Nath, Delhi; No. 154, dated the 22nd September 1910.
notes: Urdu life of Madame Blavatsky by a Theosophist colonial engineer — global esoteric movement in vernacular print.

author: Muhammad Baqir, Maulvi
title: Tazkirah-i-Mulla Majlisi
gloss: A brief account of the life of Mulla Muhammad Baqir Majlisi, a Shia theologian of repute, who was born in Persia in 1638 A. D.

author: Muhammad Said Ahmad, Munshi
title: Hayat-i-Salih
gloss: A brief biographical sketch of Nawab Sa'dullah Khan, Prime Minister to Shah Jahan, the Mughal Emperor of India.

author: Munawwar Khan, Munshi
title: Hayat-i-Aurangzeb
gloss: A brief sketch of the life of Aurangzeb, the Mugal Emperor of India, comparing him with Akbar.
notes: Date given only as June 1910. Mughal historiography debate (Aurangzeb vs Akbar) in popular Urdu print.

author: Parkash Dev, Shridrdheya
title: Sawanih-Umri Hazrat Muhammad
notes: Dual date: 81 Bramah Sammat / May 1910 — Brahmo Samaj era dating. Hindu (Brahmo) author's biography of Muhammad, 3rd edition, 3,000 copies. [Previous edition noted in entry No. 6 at page 44 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1908.]

title: Swanih-i-Sir Syad Ahmad, Marhum
gloss: A brief account of the life of the late Sir Syad Ahmad Khan, founder of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College, Aligarh.
notes: Life of Sir Sayyid Ahmad Khan, Aligarh movement founder.
